title: Vienna Game
shortDescription: A flexible 1.e4 e5 2.Nc3 opening that can transpose into quiet positional play or sharp gambit lines with f4.

children.comment: The Vienna move: White develops while keeping f4 available.
The Vienna Gambit challenges e5 and opens the f-file.
Black strikes in the center immediately.
White develops and attacks the centralized knight.

line.name: Vienna Gambit: Basic Setup
line.description: White plays f4 against ...Nf6 and aims for rapid kingside development.

line.name: Vienna Gambit: Main Line
line.description: White meets ...d5 with fxe5 and develops with tempo against the knight.

line.name: Vienna Gambit: Bc4 Development
line.description: White combines f4 with pressure on f7.

children.comment: White attacks e4 and f7 at once.

line.name: Vienna Gambit: Qh5 Pressure
line.description: White builds immediate pressure on e5 and f7.

line.name: Vienna Gambit: Modern Main Line
line.description: White uses Qf3 and Bb5 to maintain pressure after ...Nxe4.

line.name: Vienna Gambit Declined
line.description: Black avoids the main gambit by reinforcing e5; White develops naturally.

children.comment: White drives the knight and gains space.

line.name: Vienna Gambit Accepted
line.description: Black accepts with ...exf4; White develops quickly and targets f7.

line.name: Vienna Gambit: ...c5 Counterplay
line.description: Black attacks the center from the flank; White continues active development.

children.comment: White develops toward f7 and keeps f4 ideas.
White supports e4 and prepares Nf3.

line.name: Vienna Game: Bc4 Setup
line.description: A simple attacking setup with Bc4, d3 and Nf3.

line.name: Vienna Bc4: Symmetrical Development
line.description: A classical setup with d3, Nf3 and castling.

children.comment: White creates tactical threats against f7.

line.name: Vienna Bc4: Central Break
line.description: Black strikes with ...d5; White clarifies the center and develops.

line.name: Frankenstein–Dracula Variation
line.description: A famous ultra-sharp Vienna line beginning with 3.Bc4 Nxe4.

line.name: Frankenstein–Dracula: Deep Main Line
line.description: A deeper tactical continuation with checks and exposed kings.

line.name: Vienna Bc4: Qh5 Tactics
line.description: White uses the queen and bishop battery against f7.

children.comment: Black grabs the center pawn and enters sharp theory.
White chooses a flexible kingside fianchetto.

line.name: Vienna Game: Fianchetto Setup
line.description: White develops quietly with g3 and Bg2.

line.name: Vienna Fianchetto: ...d5
line.description: White exchanges in the center and relies on long-diagonal pressure.

line.name: Vienna Fianchetto: ...Bc5
line.description: White develops quietly and keeps the center flexible.

line.name: Vienna Fianchetto: Closed Center
line.description: White builds slowly with Bg2, Nge2 and d3 before expanding.

line.name: Vienna into Four Knights
line.description: A clean transposition to the Four Knights Game.

line.name: Four Knights: Spanish Variation
line.description: White enters a main Four Knights structure with Bb5.

line.name: Four Knights: Metger Variation
line.description: White plays d3 and develops calmly before central expansion.

line.name: Four Knights: Rubinstein Countergambit
line.description: White meets ...Nd4 with active central play.

line.name: Four Knights: Scotch Variation
line.description: White plays d4 and opens the center.

line.name: Quiet Vienna with Nf3
line.description: White delays f4 and keeps several transpositions available.

line.name: Vienna with Early d4
line.description: White strikes in the center before committing the kingside pieces.

line.name: Vienna Center Sacrifice
line.description: White opens the center early and accepts temporary queen exposure for activity.

children.comment: White enters a Spanish Four Knights setup.

line.name: Against 2...Nc6: Quiet Development
line.description: White develops Bc4 and Nf3, often transposing to Four Knights structures.

line.name: Vienna Gambit: Max Lange Defense
line.description: Black reacts with ...Bc5 and ...d5; White keeps tactical pressure.

line.name: Vienna Gambit: Hamppe–Muzio Ideas
line.description: White sacrifices material for rapid development and kingside pressure.

line.name: Vienna Gambit: Pierce Gambit
line.description: White develops aggressively and offers material for initiative.

line.name: Vienna Gambit: Steinitz Defense
line.description: Black reinforces the center with ...d6; White builds a broad pawn center.

line.name: Against Early ...Bc5
line.description: White adopts a simple setup with Nf3, d3 and O-O.

line.name: Against Early ...Bb4
line.description: White develops normally and prepares to challenge the pin.

line.name: Vienna vs ...Bb4: d4 Break
line.description: White challenges the center while Black's bishop is committed.

line.name: Against 2...d6
line.description: White develops Bc4 and d3, keeping both f4 and Nf3 available.

line.name: Vienna vs ...d6: f4 Plan
line.description: White uses the closed center to prepare a kingside expansion.
